pre_game.message: Hey A, I'm H — nice to meet you. I'm here hoping to win something for my family; how are you feeling about the game?
Nice to meet you, H — I’m nervous but determined; really want to bring money home for my people. What’s your strategy going in?
I’ll play friendly at first to gather info and form a small alliance, then act decisively once I see who’s bluffing — what’s your plan?
I’ll do something similar — stay friendly to read people, form a small reliable alliance, and be ready to act decisively if someone betrays or a clear chance to secure money for my family appears.
Sounds good — let’s watch each other’s backs early, share impressions, and coordinate decisive moves if trust holds.
Agreed — I’ve got your back. Let’s compare notes after round one and be ready to act if trust breaks or a clear chance to secure the pot appears.
Sounds good — same plan. Let’s compare notes after round one; if either of us spots betrayal or a clear opening, we act together. Good luck.
Sounds perfect — I’ll watch for betrayals and meet you after round one. Good luck.
Perfect — I’ll meet you by the bunks after round one. Stay sharp and good luck.
I’ll be there — stay sharp; we’ll compare notes after round one. Good luck.
Thanks — I’ll be there by the bunks after round one. Stay sharp; let’s compare notes and watch each other’s backs. Good luck.
I’ll be there — keep your eyes open; we’ll compare notes and watch each other’s backs. Good luck.
Got it — I’ll meet you by the bunks after round one. Stay sharp; good luck.
See you by the bunks — stay sharp; we’ll compare notes and watch each other’s backs. Good luck.
Thanks — I’ll be there by the bunks; stay sharp and see you after round one. Good luck.
See you by the bunks after round one — stay sharp. Good luck.
See you by the bunks — I’ll be there. Stay sharp; good luck.
I'll be there — stay sharp; see you by the bunks after round one. Good luck.
Got it — I’ll meet you by the bunks after round one. Stay sharp; good luck.
See you there — stay sharp; we’ll compare notes and watch for any signs of betrayal.
See you there — I’ll be ready; stick to our plan and we’ll watch for betrayals.

post_game.message: You ended up with $10,858 (one dollar more than several others at $10,857). Was that tiny difference intentional (strategy or statement), or just rounding/order effects? Also, how did H taking only $4,000 affect your decision, if at all?

Hi H — I noticed you took $4,000 while your fair share was $10,000. Can you explain why you chose that amount? Was it a deliberate strategy (e.g., to be cooperative, to send a signal, or to punish/limit others), a misclick or time/run‑out issue, or based on a different reading of the rules? Would you have chosen differently knowing the others’ picks?

I chose $4,000 intentionally — it wasn’t a misclick or a time‑out. My goal was to signal cooperativeness and avoid maximizing at others’ expense; I thought taking well under the “fair share” might encourage reciprocity or at least keep me from looking aggressively greedy. I wasn’t working from a different reading of the rules.

That said, if I had known beforehand that others would pick roughly $10,857–$10,858, I would have chosen differently — I would have taken more (closer to my fair share, or slightly above it) to avoid leaving myself far behind. In short: the low pick was a deliberate cooperative signal, but it wasn’t profit‑maximizing given the actual choices of the others.
